title: Reopening Closed Defaults
subtitle: Minimal Intervention Conditions after Governance Diagnostics
core_thesis: A closed default is not reopened by diagnosis alone; reopening requires trigger recognition, cost assumption, reopening act, and persistence of re-entry.
core_formula: D*closed -> operationally open iff T_r and C_a and R_o and P_r
failure_condition: not T_r or not C_a or not R_o or not P_r implies symbolic rupture only
one_sentence: SDeltaPhi-31 defines reopening as enacted editability: the intervention sequence through which diagnosed closure becomes operationally open.
negative_constraints: Do not treat diagnosis as reopening.
Do not treat critique as intervention.
Do not treat petition as automatic reopening.
Do not treat one-time exception as persistence of re-entry.
Do not use public petition case pattern to decide policy merits.
